case_name: JXM v An NHS Trust  [2020] EWHC 919 (QB) (10 March 2020)

evidence_index.text: The claimant was born on [a date in] 2013, the second child to his mother and father, they having had a daughter, S, born on [a date in] 2012.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Within a short period of the claimant's birth, he was subject to criminal assault by his father such that when he attended the defendant's hospital on [a date in] 2013, chest x-rays showed healing rib fractures which were not non-accidentally incurred.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On 14 January 2014, at shortly after 10 to 3 in the morning, an ambulance was called to the premises and on attendance, the ambulance staff found the father attempting resuscitation of the claimant who had stopped breathing.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Life-saving treatment was administered but a CT scan revealed that he had sustained devastating brain damage at the hands of his father.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In those circumstances, the defendant Trust has admitted liability for the injuries sustained by the claimant because of its breach of duty in failing to detect the non-accidental injuries.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The injuries sustained by the claimant are described in the medical report of Dr Santosh Mordekar, consultant paediatric neurologist, who describes the claimant having sustained: brain damage, namely a diffuse hypoxic brain injury with bilateral subdural haemorrhages, acute and bilateral retinal haemorrhages; rib fractures; burst fracture of the C3 vertebrae of the spine; and a fractured rib and tibia.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: He is doubly incontinent and will probably never attain continence. He has global intellectual impairment assessed by Dr Mordekar as a severe level of intellectual functioning.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The family of the claimant includes his maternal grandmother who is a retired nurse and his maternal grandfather, as well as a maternal aunt, the elder sister to whom I have already referred, and also a younger child to whom the mother has given birth since.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The maternal grandmother has taken on the task of looking after the claimant on a fulltime basis and she has given up her job as a nurse in order to do so.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: I do make the order sought and I simply comment that whilst I do not believe that the concerns of the defendant are likely to result in practice in either of the parents gaining from their criminal activities.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient was found not breathing by father, resuscitation attempted
past_medical_history: Rib fractures noted previously but not investigated

requestables.name: CT Brain
requestables.description: CT scan of the brain to assess for injury

reveal.result_summary: Diffuse hypoxic brain injury with bilateral subdural haemorrhages
reveal.clinical_significance: Indicates severe brain injury likely due to non-accidental trauma
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt protective custody and further investigation

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient initially presented with rib fractures

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient presented with rib fractures not previously investigated
decision_points.prompt: What should have been done upon initial presentation with rib fractures?

options.description: Order full skeletal survey and report to child protection services
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard care for suspected non-accidental injury
Failed to recognize signs of non-accidental injury

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant discharged patient without further investigation
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Failed to identify rib fractures as non-accidental

expected_action_court.description: Full skeletal survey and child protection referral should have been done
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence and standard protocols for suspected abuse

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failed to follow standard protocols for suspected non-accidental injury
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Breach of duty in failing to detect non-accidental injuries

patient_outcome.description: Patient sustained severe brain injury and multiple disabilities
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Cerebral palsy, epilepsy, severe intellectual impairment

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to detect non-accidental injuries
Causation established - earlier intervention could have prevented further harm

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Investigation could have led to protective custody

court_findings.legal_significance: Breach of duty in failing to detect non-accidental injuries
